steps.title: Find the slope of line s
steps.content: Rewrite $10x+2y=0$ as $$2y=-10x$$ $$y=-5x.$$ Therefore line $s$ has slope $-5$.

steps.title: Build the perpendicular line
steps.content: A perpendicular line has slope $\frac{1}{5}$, the negative reciprocal of $-5$. Since line $t$ passes through $(7,0)$, write $$y=\frac{1}{5}(x-7).$$

steps.title: Match the point with y = 6/5
steps.content: The point $(k,\frac{6}{5})$ lies on line $t$, so set $y=\frac{6}{5}$: $$\frac{6}{5}=\frac{1}{5}(k-7).$$ Multiplying by $5$ gives $6=k-7$, so $k=13$.

steps.title: Confirm the value
steps.content: Substitute $k=13$ into the line equation: $$y=\frac{1}{5}(13-7)=\frac{6}{5}.$$ Therefore the value of $k$ is 13.
